draft.action_intent: Submit the search query entered in the top navigation search bar to find results on Wikipedia
draft.notes: This is the header/navbar search button (id=4), distinct from the in-page search button (id=11). Both trigger the same search action but from different locations on the page.

draft.action_intent: Submit the search query entered in the main search results page input to execute or refine a Wikipedia search
draft.notes: This is the in-page search submit button below the main search input (id=10). Functionally equivalent to id=4 but associated with the body-level search form.

draft.action_intent: Toggle or expand the advanced search options panel to refine search parameters such as namespaces or filters
draft.notes: This is an anchor styled as a button (role=button). It likely expands/collapses an advanced search panel in-place rather than navigating to a new page.

draft.action_intent: Enter or modify a search query in the top navigation bar search field
draft.notes: Header-level search input (type=search). Typing here does not commit any action until the user submits. May show autocomplete suggestions.

draft.action_intent: Enter or modify the search query in the main search results page input field to refine the current search
draft.notes: This is the body-level combobox search input pre-filled with 'brain agent'. The combobox role suggests it may offer autocomplete/dropdown suggestions as the user types.

draft.action_intent: Navigate to the Wikipedia Main Page
draft.notes: Likely the Wikipedia logo/wordmark link in the top-left corner (empty accessible name, links to Main_Page). Standard site-wide navigation element.
